summary: Designs, automates, and optimizes workflows across the CI‑OS to reduce friction, eliminate waste, and ensure reliable process execution.
responsibilities: Design automated workflows that support CI‑OS processes, including improvement pipelines, deviation handling, and approval flows
Implement workflow logic using orchestration platforms, low-code tools, or automation engines
Collaborate with Process Engineers to translate process maps into executable digital workflows
Monitor workflow performance, latency, and failure rates to ensure reliability
Automate repetitive tasks and handoffs to reduce waste and improve cycle time
Integrate workflows with dashboards, data pipelines, and digital-twin systems
Maintain workflow documentation, version control, and change history
Ensure workflows comply with quality, safety, and governance standards
Identify automation opportunities and propose enhancements to CI‑OS leadership
Support exception handling and escalation logic for human review queues
authorities: Define workflow logic and routing rules
Integrate workflows with CI‑OS systems and APIs
Pause or reroute workflows during incidents or failures
decisionRights: Selection of automation tools and orchestration patterns
Definition of workflow triggers and routing logic
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of automation opportunities to eliminate waste
Support for frontline and supervisor workflow improvements
cqiCapabilities: Integration of CQI metrics into workflow logic

constraints: All workflows must comply with quality and safety governance
Automation changes require documentation and version control
High-risk workflows require Quality Manager approval
